name: machine-timer
description: Hardware timer for periodic or one-shot callback execution. ESP32 provides 4 virtual timers.
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific timer interface.

constructor_params.name: id
constructor_params.description: Timer ID. ESP32: 0-3 for virtual timers. Use any unused ID.

parameters.name: freq
parameters.description: Frequency in Hz. Use EITHER freq OR period, not both.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Period in milliseconds. Use EITHER freq OR period, not both.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Function called on timer event. Receives 1 arg: the Timer object.

callback_signature_warning.title: Timer callback MUST accept exactly 1 argument
callback_signature_warning.explanation: The callback function receives the Timer object as its only argument. A callback with no arguments will crash.

example:
from machine import Timer
from micropython import alloc_emergency_exception_buf

alloc_emergency_exception_buf(100)  # recommended for timer callbacks

def tick(timer):
    print('1 second passed')

timer = Timer(0)
timer.init(period=1000, mode=Timer.PERIODIC, callback=tick)

# Later:
# timer.deinit()  # stop timer
esp32_notes: Timer callbacks execute in interrupt context — keep them short and non-blocking.
Cannot allocate memory inside a timer callback (no object creation).
Always call alloc_emergency_exception_buf(100) at startup for timer debugging.
Virtual timers use the ESP32's high-resolution timer (1µs resolution).
